key_insight: An educational proof assistant can improve how first-year students construct proofs and make their reasoning more explicit, with benefits that may transfer to paper proofs.
engineering_relevance: For CS/math education tools, this suggests controlled natural language proof systems can support learning outcomes beyond the software interface itself.
method: Quasi-experiment in an introductory proof course
Mixed setting: multiple teachers, study programs, and Dutch-English language context
Used in-class observation, surveys, and student proof/performance analysis
evidence_signal: The authors combine observational, survey, and performance data and report suggestive improvements in proof quality and grades, but not causal proof due to self-selection.
watch_item: Because students self-selected into using Waterproof, the results may reflect selection effects rather than the tool alone.
paper_title: The Educational Proof Assistant Waterproof in an Introductory Proof Course: Proof Construction and Learning Processes
